title: Upstart (UPST) Q1 2026 Earnings Transcript
text: Upstart delivered strong origination and revenue growth across its product suite in fiscal Q1 2026 (period ended March 31, 2026), with rapid expansion in auto and home products and continued strength in core personal loans. Key Growth Metrics: Originations were up 61% to $3.4 billion and transaction volume was up 77% to 425,356 loans, driving total revenue up by 44% to $308.2 million, which topped estimates at $303 million. However, profit declined marginally with originations growing 61% year over year and revenue growing 44%. Profitability Concerns: Despite the strong top-line growth, there were margin pressures. Adjusted EBITDA margin narrowed from 20% to 13%, and GAAP loss per share widened from $0.03 to $0.07. Sales and marketing spending nearly doubled to $104.4 million. Guidance and Outlook: The company maintained its revenue guidance for the full year at $1.4 billion, up 34.5% from 2025, and adjusted EBITDA of $294 million, up 28%. Management confirmed that profitability was temporarily impacted by front-loaded expenses, product mix, and seasonality, but projected sequential margin improvement through 2026.
